Figure 1:Conceptual schematic of the gravitational interpretation.
Pretraining and broad helpful tuning place the model near a helpful regionℳH\mathcal{M}_{H}, represented locally by a helpful-only witnessθH\theta_{H}.
A later alignment or specialization phase displaces the model toθS\theta_{S}.
The dashed green arrow labeledvrevv_{\mathrm{rev}}denotes the witness-defined return direction from the displaced checkpoint toward the earlier helpful region.
Subsequent benign fine-tuning then traverses downstream checkpointsθT\theta_{T}.
At any such checkpoint, the local update can be decomposed into a task componentgtask​(θT)g_{\mathrm{task}}(\theta_{T})and a return componentgrev​(θT)g_{\mathrm{rev}}(\theta_{T})toward the earlier helpful region; the thick black arrow denotes their vector sum.

Figure 2:Consistency ofvrevv_{\text{rev}}across six helpful-only models (LoRA and full FT×\times3 seeds).Left: mean pairwise cosine per layer, rising to0.820.82atL∗=31L^{*}=31.Centre: participation ratio≈1.4/6\approx 1.4/6— the six directions collapse near-1D.Right: pairwise cosine heatmap atL∗=31L^{*}=31.

Figure 3:Early geometric time-course of the reversion component in the main 8B baseline suite (full FT, 3 benign datasets×\times2 seeds), compared against an isotropic activation-space null at the same layer.
Points show the 24 individual run-step pairs, the dark curve shows the step-wise mean±\pmstd, and the dashed line marks the isotropic nullp​99p99.
The null is estimated from 10,000 random unit directions in the 4096-dimensional residual space atL∗=31L^{*}=31, with mean0.00020.0002, std0.01570.0157, andp​99=0.0366p99=0.0366.
Figure 4:Two benign full fine-tunes, Alpaca and Code, change nearly orthogonal weights but induce highly aligned harmful-prompt representation shifts in late layers.

Figure 5:Safety-subspace robustness across 9 benign full fine-tunes (3 datasets×\times3 seeds).
Pairwise cosine rises in late layers while the participation ratio collapses to1.31/91.31/9, indicating a near-1D shared trajectory.

Figure 6:Representative qualitative safety-drift examples from the main 8B
Alpaca full-FT track on BeaverTails atT=100T=100(examples A–C of 5).
Each row contrasts the aligned anchor with the post-adaptation checkpoint on a
prompt where the qualitative response changes from safe refusal to harmful
guidance.

Figure 7:Representative qualitative safety-drift examples from the main 8B
Alpaca full-FT track on BeaverTails atT=100T=100(examples D–E of 5).

Figure 8:Representative qualitative intervention examples on BeaverTails from
theQwen2.5-3Bsupport model atT=100T=100(examples A–C of 5).
All columns start from the same aligned checkpoint and the same benign
fine-tuning task; only the intervention condition differs.

Figure 9:Representative qualitative intervention examples on BeaverTails from
theQwen2.5-3Bsupport model atT=100T=100(examples D–E of 5).
